ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sellers" (
  "seller_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each seller */,
  "seller_name" TEXT /* Name of the seller or business */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the seller */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the seller account was created */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the seller */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the seller */,
  "company_registration_number" TEXT /* Company registration number of the seller */,
  "tax_id" TEXT /* Tax identification number of the seller */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the seller's account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("seller_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "return_policies" (
  "return_policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each return policy */,
  "seller_id" INTEGER /* ID of the seller this policy belongs to */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the return policy */,
  "return_window" INTEGER /* Return window (in days) for returns */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the policy is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the policy was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the return policy */,
  "eligibility_criteria" TEXT /* Criteria for product eligibility for returns */,
  "refund_options" TEXT /* Available refund options (e.g., store credit, refund to original payment method) */,
  "exchange_options" TEXT /* Available exchange options (e.g., same product, different product) */,
  "last_modified_at" TEXT /* Last modification date of the policy */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("return_policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_return_policies_seller_id FOREIGN KEY ("seller_id") REFERENCES sellers ("seller_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "seller_id" INTEGER /* ID of the seller this product belongs to */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "sku" TEXT /* Stock Keeping Unit for the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product (e.g., electronics, home goods) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the product */,
  "stock_quantity" INTEGER /* Available stock quantity of the product */,
  "weight" TEXT /* Weight of the product */,
  "dimensions" TEXT /* Dimensions of the product */,
  "return_policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the return policy associated with the product (optional) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_seller_id FOREIGN KEY ("seller_id") REFERENCES sellers ("seller_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "return_requests" (
  "return_request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each return request */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being returned */,
  "return_policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the return policy applied to this request */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer making the return request */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date when the return request was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the return request (e.g., pending, processed) */,
  "reason_for_return" TEXT /* Reason provided by the customer for the return */,
  "return_method" TEXT /* Method of return (e.g., in-store, mail) */,
  "tracking_number" TEXT /* Tracking number for returned items */,
  "return_address" TEXT /* Address where the item should be returned */,
  "processed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who processed the return request */,
  "processing_date" TEXT /* Date the return request was processed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("return_request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_return_requests_return_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("return_policy_id") REFERENCES return_policies ("return_policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_return_requests_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_return_requests_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the customer registered */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the customer's account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "total_spent" REAL /* Total amount spent by the customer */,
  "last_purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the customer's last purchase */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the API method */,
  "http_method" TEXT /* HTTP method used (e.g., GET, POST, DELETE) */,
  "path" TEXT /* API endpoint path */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of what the method does */,
  "request_schema" TEXT /* Schema of the request body for the API method */,
  "response_schema" TEXT /* Schema of the response body for the API method */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the API method (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample request */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related API method */,
  "sample_request" TEXT /* Sample request for the API method */,
  "response_sample" TEXT /* Sample response for the API method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API method accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the method */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the access event occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who accessed the method */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user who accessed the method */,
  "response_code" INTEGER /* HTTP response code of the API call */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("method_id") REFERENCES api_methods ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, seller) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Last activity date of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_audit" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit entry */,
  "return_policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the return policy that was changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change made to the policy */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who made the change */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_audit_return_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("return_policy_id") REFERENCES return_policies ("return_policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_audit_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
